zh.lifeSummary: 當Rowland Taylor於1510年10月6日出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德洛斯賀波利時，他的父親John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst32歲，母親Susan Rowland29歲。他於1525年與Margaret Tyndale在英國諾圖波藍德洛斯賀波利結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和10個女兒的父母。他去世於1555年2月9日在英國素福爾可哈德利享年44歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可哈德利。

nameMeanings.name: Taylor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: occupational name for a tailor, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English taillour ‘tailor’ (Old French tailleor, tailleur; Late Latin taliator, from taliare ‘to cut’). The surname is extremely common in Britain and Ireland. In North America, it has absorbed equivalents from other languages, many of which are also common among Ashkenazic Jews, for example German Schneider and Hungarian Szabo . It is also very common among African Americans.
In some cases also an Americanized form of French   Terrien   ‘owner of a farmland’ or of its altered forms, such as   Therrien   and   Terrian  .

variants.name: Tayloe
meanings: Altered form of English, Scottish, and Irish   Taylor  .

variants.name: Schneider
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Franz, Erwin, Fritz, Dieter, Manfred, Heinz, Alois, Ewald, Helmut.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a tailor, literally ‘cutter’, from Middle High German snīder, German Schneider, Yiddish shnayder. The same term was sometimes used to denote a woodcutter. This surname is also established in many other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), the Netherlands, Hungary, Poland, and Czechia. In Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia it is also established in Slavicized forms (see 2 below). In part, Schneider is a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname, originating from the Kočevsko region in Lower Carniola, Slovenia (see Kocevar ). Compare Sneider 1, Snider 1, and Snyder 1.
Germanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Šnajdr or Šnajder, and Czech Šneidr, Šneider, Šnejdr, and Šnejder, surnames of German origin (see above).

variants.name: Tailor
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Pravin, Dilip, Mahesh, Sanjay, Kanchan, Kaushik, Manilal, Mohanlal, Navnit, Shailesh, Ajay, Alpana.
Indian (Gujarat and Mumbai): mainly Parsi occupational name from the English word tailor.

nameMeanings.name: Rowland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roland  , or a transferred use of the surname derived from that given name in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Roland
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + land ‘land, territory’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. In Old French literature it is borne by a legendary Frankish hero, a vassal of Charlemagne, whose exploits are told in the Chanson de Roland. The subject of the poem is Roland's death at the Battle of Roncesvalles in the Pyrenees in 778 , while protecting the rearguard of the Frankish army on its retreat from Spain. Roland is depicted in literature and legend as headstrong and impulsive. His devoted friendship with the prudent Oliver is also legendary.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Rowland Taylor was born on 6 October 1510, in Rothbury, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst, was 32 and his mother, Susan Rowland, was 29. He married Margaret Tyndale in 1525, in Rothbury, Northumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 10 daughters. He died on 9 February 1555, in Hadleigh, Suffolk, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Hadleigh, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rowland Taylor est né le 6 octobre 1510, à Rothbury, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Susan Rowland, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Tyndale en 1525, à Rothbury, Northumberland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 10 filles. Il est décédé le 9 février 1555, à Hadleigh, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Hadleigh, Suffolk, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Rowland Taylorが1510年10月6日にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドロスブリーで生まれた時，父John H Taylor of Shadoxhurstは32歳，母Susan Rowlandは29歳であった。1525年にイギリスノースウンバーランドロスブリーでMargaret Tyndaleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と10人の娘の親であった。彼は1555年2月9日にイギリススフォルクハドレイグで44歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクハドレイグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Rowland Taylor(이)가 1510년 10월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 로스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Susan Rowland(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1525년 영국 노르스벌랜드 로스버이에서 Margaret Tyndale(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1555년 2월 9일 영국 수폴크 하들리에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 하들리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rowland Taylor родился 6 октября 1510 г. в местности ротбури, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Susan Rowland, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Tyndale в 1525 г. в местности ротбури, нортумбирланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 10 дочерей. Он умер 9 февраля 1555 г. в местности хадлиг, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности хадлиг, суффолк, Англия.
